commit.message:
Add parallel subprocess support for GPG password testing

- Add --workers/-w option (default: 4) to control parallel GPG workers
- Extract try_password() function for individual GPG decrypt attempts
- Use ThreadPoolExecutor to test multiple passwords concurrently
- Stop early when correct password is found, cancelling pending futures
- Update existing GPG tests with -w 1 for deterministic behavior
- Add unit tests for try_password and parallel execution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message: feat: Lighthouse 100% — SEO sur 37 pages, a11y formulaires, og:image/twitter, JSON-LD @graph, WebP (#1205) * Initial plan * fix: Lighthouse 100 - SPA routing, broken images, chunking, SEO, manifest, routes Critical: - Delete public/index.html (was overwriting Vite build → blank page, NO_FCP) - Fix 404.html path redirect (slice(0,2) keeps /akiprisaye-web/ base) - Add path-restore script to index.html (reads ?p= before React boots) Performance: - Vite function-based manualChunks: main bundle 604KB → 216KB (-64%) - Firebase (347KB), charts (372KB), leaflet (200KB), icons (38KB) isolated - Add preconnect hints for Unsplash CDN SEO: - Fix robots.txt paths (add /akiprisaye-web/ prefix) - Fix CSP hash for inline script - Improve JSON-LD structured data (WebApplication) - Add hreflang, keywords, author, og:image dimensions - Extend sitemap.xml with 40+ missing routes Bug fixes: - Fix 6 broken Unsplash 404 URLs (photo-1542838132-2b1a08e4b1a0) - Fix hero background 404 (photo-1605217613423) - Fix manifest share_target.action (relative path) - Fix TypeScript: add territory? to ImageSearchInput - Add missing /recherche-produits route in App.tsx UI: - HeroPhoneMockup: two-column hero with phone illustration (desktop) - ComparateursHub: 5 new sections (Scanners, Calculateurs, Observatoire, IA, Citoyen) Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com> * feat: innovative 2D/3D UI — aurora bg, particles, tilt cards, flip stats, scroll reveal, neon glow New effects (all respect prefers-reduced-motion): - Aurora gradient orbs: 4 animated radial blobs behind hero (CSS only) - Floating particles: 12 rising bubbles with random sizes/speeds (CSS only) - Morphing blob: CSS clip-path border-radius animation for depth - 3D tilt card: useTilt3D hook drives rotateX/Y via CSS vars on mousemove (touch+reduced-motion safe, disabled automatically) - 3D flip stat cards: FlipStatCard component with CSS preserve-3d, shows context on hover/focus via rotateY(180deg) - Neon glow CTA: conic-gradient spinning border on search button hover - Scan border: animated gradient border on focused search form - Scroll reveal: useScrollReveal IntersectionObserver hook adds .revealed class to .reveal elements for smooth entrance animations - Live badge: pulsing green dot before "Données en direct" - Gradient text: CSS background-clip text utility - Enhanced glassmorphism: mouse-position shine overlay via radial-gradient and CSS custom props (--shine-x/y) Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com> * fix: Lighthouse 100% gaps — CSP hash, @property angle, contain, CLS, hero preload 1. _headers: update sha256 to match current inline path-restore script (was sha256-3ffE..., now sha256-mZvV... — Cloudflare would have blocked the SPA routing script with the stale hash → broken navigation) 2. innovations-3d.css: add @property --angle declaration so neon-spin conic-gradient animation interpolates smoothly (without it the border jumps at each frame instead of rotating) 3. innovations-3d.css: add contain: strict to .aurora-bg and .particles-container to isolate paint area; reduce aurora blur 80→60px 4. LiveNewsFeed.tsx: add width="400" height="140" to article thumbnail so browser reserves space before CSS is parsed → prevents CLS 5. index.html: add for the Unsplash hero CSS background-image so browser discovers it early 6. ModerationDashboard.tsx: replace alt="" with descriptive alt text Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com> * feat: minor improvements — SEOHead og:image/twitter, JSON-LD @graph, WebP, a11y, ESLint clean 1. SEOHead.tsx — enrichi avec ogImage, ogImageAlt, ogImageWidth/Height, twitterCard ('summary'|'summary_large_image'), noIndex, og:url, og:locale; defaults: icon-512.png 512×512, twitter:card summary_large_image 2. index.html — JSON-LD @graph : WebApplication + Organization + WebSite avec SearchAction (potentialAction), areaServed pour les 5 DOM, logo ImageObject, @id cross-references 3. imageAssets.ts — 161 URLs Unsplash : auto=format → auto=format&fm=webp (–20–40% taille, même qualité visuelle); hero CSS + preload mis à jour 4. HeroImage.tsx — avec + fallback; helper toWebP() pour les URLs Unsplash 5. Accessibilité : - StoreRanking : aria-label descriptif sur chaque bouton enseigne - TerritoryPriceChart : aria-label sur les boutons role="tab" - TerritorySignal : aria-label + aria-pressed sur les boutons territoire; aria-label sur "Scanner un ticket" - DataConfidenceCard : aria-label dynamique sur bouton toggle explication - FlipStatCard : role="button", aria-label enrichi, handler Enter/Space, classe CSS flip-card-inner--flipped pour JS-triggered flip - Home.tsx : retrait aria-label redondant sur badge-live span 6. ESLint 0 erreurs / 0 warnings : - DLCAntigaspi.tsx : let list → const list - useScrollReveal.ts : retrait eslint-disable-next-line obsolète - ShareVictory.tsx : console.log('Share cancelled') → catch silent - PremiumGuard.tsx : console.log('Open subscription page') retiré - map/examples.tsx : console.log('Store selected') retiré 7. CSS mineur : - will-change: border-radius → will-change: transform sur .blob-decoration - flip-card-inner--flipped selector ajouté pour keyboard flip Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com> * feat: SEO 100% — SEOHead sur 37 pages sitemap, fragments JSX corrigés Toutes les pages du sitemap reçoivent maintenant un et <meta name="description"> via le composant SEOHead enrichi. commit.message:
Remove IL trimming (breaks WinForms UI Automation)

Partial trimming stripped UIAutomation interop types that WinForms
needs at runtime for accessibility events (ListBox focus,
ContextMenuStrip close), causing TypeLoadException crashes.

WinForms is not trim-safe. Reverted to untrimmed self-contained
publish. Installer returns to 63.5 MB.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Move config/log to AppData for Program Files compatibility

App data (config.json, block-from-recent.log) was stored next to the EXE,
which fails silently when installed to Program Files (write-protected).

Add AppPaths.cs: centralizes all paths to %AppData%\BlockFromRecent\.
Update ConfigManager and Log to use AppPaths.
Update installer to clean AppData dir on uninstall.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: block-from-recent
repository.description: Windows tray utility that automatically removes unwanted files from Recent Files based on user-defined exclusion rules.

commit.message:
Replace COM interop with native .lnk binary parser

Remove all COM interop (IShellLinkW, ShellLink class, WIN32_FIND_DATAW)
and replace with a pure managed .NET parser that reads the MS-SHLLINK
binary format directly via File.ReadAllBytes + BitConverter.

Supports:
- Local file paths (ANSI and Unicode)
- UNC/network paths via CommonNetworkRelativeLink
- Path suffixes for network shares

This eliminates:
- COM threading issues (STA requirement, SynchronizationContext)
- Native memory corruption risk from marshaling mismatches
- Hard crashes that bypass managed exception handlers
- Dependency on Windows COM infrastructure

170 .lnk files now parse in ~9ms vs hanging/crashing with COM.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial implementation of Block From Recent

System tray utility for Windows that watches Recent Files folder and
automatically removes entries matching user-defined exclusion rules.

Features:
- FileSystemWatcher-based real-time monitoring of Recent folder
- Path prefix and glob pattern exclusion rules
- COM interop .lnk shortcut target resolution
- WinForms system tray app with settings dialog
- Startup scan of existing recent files
- Auto-start with Windows via Registry
- Single-instance guard via named Mutex
- JSON config file for exclusion rules
- MIT licensed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: block-from-recent
repository.description: Windows tray utility that automatically removes unwanted files from Recent Files based on user-defined exclusion rules.

commit.message:
Add file logging, fix UI layout, remove emoji from menus

- Add Log.cs: file-based logger (block-from-recent.log next to EXE)
- Add global exception handlers in Program.cs (ThreadException + UnhandledException)
- Add logging throughout TrayApplicationContext, RecentFileCleaner, SettingsForm
- Rewrite SettingsForm layout: use AutoScaleDimensions(96,96) + AutoScaleMode.Dpi
  with explicit coordinates at 96dpi baseline — WinForms auto-scales from there
- Increase form ClientSize to 600x440 with proper spacing for all controls
- Rewrite PromptInput dialog with same DPI-aware approach
- Remove emoji characters from context menu items (rendering issues)
- Add per-file try/catch with logging in test and scan operations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor runPerfTests to reduce function length

Extract long function into smaller helper functions:
- initializeGPUBackend: GPU setup and configuration
- runScenarioBenchmarks: Iterate through test scenarios
- benchmarkScenario: Run single scenario benchmark
- printBenchmarkResults: Display frame stats
- printMemoryStatistics: Display memory stats

Results:
- runPerfTests: 79 lines → 9 lines (88% reduction)
- Cyclomatic complexity: 6 → 2 (66% improvement)
- Overall complexity: 9.3 → 3.1 (66% improvement)

Resolves AUDIT.md LOW finding line 75.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: lazy-load user avatars and event covers (#66)

## Summary

Replaces inline base64 images in list views with lazy-loaded per-item
components that fetch from the new dedicated backend endpoints.

### Changes

**API layer**
- `api/users.ts` — `getUserAvatar(userId)` → `GET /profile/{id}/avatar`
- `api/events.ts` — `getEventCover(eventId)` → `GET /events/{id}/cover`

**New components**
- `components/user/UserAvatar.vue` — fetches avatar on mount, shows
`UserCircleIcon` until loaded
- `components/event/EventCover.vue` — fetches cover on mount, shows gray
placeholder until loaded

**Updated components**
- `components/user/UserTable.vue` — replaced inline `v-if="user.avatar"`
img block with `<UserAvatar>`
- `components/event/EventTable.vue` — fixed prop type `Event[]` →
`EventListItem[]`, replaced `event.cover` img with `<EventCover>`

### Result
List pages load fast (no images in the initial payload). Images fill in
asynchronously per row as components mount.

### Related
Backend PR: iu-alumni/iu-alumni-backend feat/lazy-image-endpoints

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: send done event from /command/stream endpoint

The /command/stream SSE endpoint was missing the final 'done' event
with the response text. The client's processStream() waits for a
done event to extract the final response - without it, the stream
ends with an empty result.

Also filter out duplicate 'done' events from onProgress callback
to match /voice/stream behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Phase 7 — Tooling

- Formatter: AST-based code formatter with style rules (src/fmt.rs)

commit.message:
fix: root cause fix for LLM task status noise in chat

Root cause: In the final tool-use loop round (no tool_calls), the LLM
outputs task status narration like '[image_generation] 任务状态：正在生成中...'
as regular content. The code already suppresses content in intermediate
rounds (when tool_calls exist), but the final round content was passed
through unfiltered.

Fix: Add deterministic noise stripping in the backend content pipeline
(alongside existing [Previously generated results] stripping). Lines
matching [capability] 任务状态：... are stripped before streaming to
frontend and persisting to DB.

Reverted: previous frontend regex filter and system prompt instructions
that were treating symptoms (兜底), not root cause.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: write Linux perf reports to /output so they survive the container

PERF_REPORT_DIR=/project/tests was wrong: in cibuildwheel's manylinux
container /project is mounted read-only for the test step, so conftest.py
silently failed to write the perf report (OSError caught).

For non-Linux platforms cibuildwheel runs natively and {package} expands
to the workspace source dir, so conftest __file__ resolves to the source
tests/ — this is why macOS/Windows reports always worked.

Fix:
- Set PERF_REPORT_DIR=/output in both Linux package jobs; /output is
  bind-mounted read-write and docker-cp'd back to wheelhouse/ after the run
- Update artifact upload paths to wheelhouse/perf_report.* accordingly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix climber transpiler bugs: Mul handler + stelem self-referencing arrays (#156)

* Update climber sample GIF to reflect PR #155 transpiler fixes

The compound expression handling fixes from PR #155 (EmitOamSprDecsp4,
dup cascade, oam_spr arg scanning) now produce correct 6502 code for
the climber sample. The stale NuGet package was preventing the sample
from picking up these fixes.

The game now correctly renders:
- 4+ actors with proper metasprite tiles
- Score display with computed tile indices (0x30 + (score >> 4))
- OAM offset properly aligned (mod4=0), preventing oam_hide_rest hang
- 18 visible sprites at runtime (4 metasprites + 2 score digits)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Mul handler: detect ldloc-loaded locals for runtime multiply

The Mul handler only checked _runtimeValueInA for ASL shift optimization,
but WriteLdloc doesn't set that flag. This caused ldloc;ldc;mul patterns
(e.g. gap * 16) to:
1. Emit orphaned JSR pusha calls that leaked C stack bytes
2. Skip the ASL shift multiplication entirely

Added _lastLoadedLocalIndex detection to the Mul handler (matching the
pattern used by AND, DIV, SHR, SHL, and other operators). When a local
variable is in A, the handler now removes the unnecessary pusha+LDA
instructions emitted by WriteLdc and emits proper ASL shifts.

This fixes the climber sample crash: 4 orphaned pusha calls per frame
leaked 4 bytes/frame on the C software stack, eventually overwriting
FamiTone2 BSS at \ and causing an infinite loop in the music
engine's NMI handler.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update climber screenshots with fixed rendering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stelem self-referencing array update pattern

HandleStelemI1 now correctly handles arr[i] = arr[i] ± N patterns
(e.g., actor_x[pi] = (byte)(actor_x[pi] - 1)). Previously, these
fell through to the constant-store fallback, storing a literal value
instead of modifying the existing array element.

The fix adds a new code path that detects when sourceArray1Idx matches
targetArrayLocalIdx and emits LDX idx; LDA arr,X; SEC/CLC; SBC/ADC N;
STA arr,X — proper read-modify-write indexed addressing.

This fixes player movement (LEFT/RIGHT) and independent enemy movement
in the climber sample.

Also fixed stale NuGet packaging: dotnet pack requires -c Debug to use
the Debug configuration DLL (defaults to Release otherwise).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update climber GIF with working movement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update climber GIF using ANESE for consistent sizing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Mul handler: remove only LDA #const, not LDA \

WriteLdloc emits LDA Absolute which sets LastLDA=false, so WriteLdc
only emits LDA #constant (no pusha). RemoveLastInstructions(2) was
incorrectly removing the local load as well. Changed to remove 1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add regression test for Mul handler RemoveLastInstructions bug

The test verifies that ldloc;ldc;mul preserves the LDA  instruction.
With RemoveLastInstructions(2), the local load was incorrectly removed,
leaving ASL shifts operating on a stale accumulator value from pal_col.
The test asserts that LDA Absolute (AD) appears immediately before the
16-bit shift setup (LDX #0, STX , ASL A, ROL ).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add encounter library data and complete all Express route files

- Add encounterLibrary.ts with 34 pre-built encounters spanning all
  difficulty levels (trivial to boss) and types (combat/social/mixed)
  for party levels 1-20+. Includes getEncountersByDifficulty() and
  getEncountersForLevel() helpers.

- Complete characters.ts router with CRUD endpoints and /:id/stats
  endpoint returning derived KPIs (win rate, avg damage, etc.)

- Complete encounters.ts router with CRUD + /library endpoint
  combining static encounter library with DB library encounters.
  Level filters use inclusive overlap semantics (minLevel <= query,
  maxLevel >= query) to find encounters suitable for a given level.

- Complete combat.ts router with session start (initiative rolls,
  participant setup), action handling (attack/spell/heal/dodge/dash),
  automatic victory/defeat detection, and session end/abandon.

- Complete reference.ts router with read-only endpoints for all game
  data: species, classes, backgrounds, spells, equipment, feats,
  monsters; each with name lookup and optional query filters.

- Complete dashboard.ts router with /characters KPI summary and
  /overview aggregate stats (totals, combat summary, class and level
  distributions).

commit.message:
feat: add dedicated avatar and cover image endpoints (#69)

## Summary

Adds lightweight dedicated endpoints for fetching user avatars and event
covers, enabling lazy-loading on the frontend.

### New endpoints
- `GET /profile/{user_id}/avatar` → `{"avatar": "base64..." | null}`
- `GET /events/{event_id}/cover` → `{"cover": "base64..." | null}`

### Why
List endpoints were refactored to exclude `avatar`/`cover` fields for
performance. These new per-item image endpoints let the frontend
lazy-load images after the page renders, keeping list payloads small.

### Changes
- `app/schemas/profile.py` — new `AvatarResponse` schema
- `app/schemas/event.py` — new `CoverResponse` schema
- `app/api/routes/profile/get_other_profile.py` — new `GET
/{user_id}/avatar` route
- `app/api/routes/events/get_cover.py` — new `GET /{event_id}/cover`
route (registered before `/{event_id}` for correct precedence)

### Related
Frontend PR: iu-alumni/iu-alumni-frontend feat/lazy-image-loading

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: plugin agents use folder/agent ID format for CLI --agent flag (#516)

* fix: plugin agents use folder/agent ID format for CLI --agent flag

Plugin agents in installed-plugins/ with agency.json manifests now get
IDs prefixed with the plugin folder name (e.g., dev-team/dev-team instead
of just dev-team). This matches the Copilot CLI's expected --agent format.

Also discovers ALL agents from a plugin's agents/ directory, not just
the entry-point agent. The PluginManifest now includes allAgentPaths.

Closes #515

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add missing allAgentPaths to test PluginManifest

commit.message:
install-k3s-server: disable built-in servicelb (conflicts with MetalLB)

K3s klipper ServiceLB and MetalLB cannot run simultaneously. Klipper
prevents MetalLB from setting up iptables DNAT rules for LoadBalancer IPs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: bump to v4.0.0-alpha.13

Consolidates all daemon reliability work (P0-P3):
- P0: idle timeout, request IDs, session cleanup, client timeouts
- P1: health tracking, uptime, atomic state, file lock
- P2: session_create, daemon_config, daemon_logs + typed ErrorCode
- P3: dynamic runtime dir, stale PID cleanup, socket perms, per-cmd timeouts

Full E2E verified: 18/18 daemon tests pass.
7/7 unit tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
